IBM.bottom_line: IBM enters Q1 2026 riding the strongest momentum in over a decade. FY2025 delivered $67.54B revenue (+7.6% YoY), $10.59B net income (+76% YoY), and $14.7B free cash flow -- a decade high. Management guided FY2026 to >5% constant-currency revenue growth and $15.7B FCF, above consensus expectations. The GenAI book of business stands at $12.5B cumulative (up from $9.5B QoQ), with software comprising >$2B and consulting >$10.5B. Q4 2025 was remarkable: revenue up 12% YoY (9% CC), software up 11%, infrastructure up 17% (Z17 mainframe cycle), and consulting grew modestly but with AI bookings accelerating. The stock has outperformed the S&P 500 by ~

bull_case.thesis_headline: Prior Quarter

pushes_higher.text: Software sustainability: Can software maintain 10%+ growth in Q1 after Q4's 11%? Red Hat guided mid-single-digit growth -- any deceleration below 5% would concern investors. Conversely, AI-driven demand for watsonx and automation could sustain double-digit software growth.

Consulting growth inflection: Consulting grew only ~3% in Q4. Management expects GenAI to be a consulting accelerator. Q1 will test whether the $3.6B GenAI ARR in consulting translates to revenue acceleration or whether macro headwinds are slowing enterprise spending.

Z17 mainframe cycle: Q4 infrastructure was up 17% on Z17 strength, outpacing the Z16 cycle. Q1 typically sees strong carryover from a new mainframe launch. Any deceleration below 10% would raise questions about cycle longevity.

VRNS.bottom_line: VRNS reports Q1 FY2026 tonight AMC at $25.38 (~60% off Oct 2025 highs of $63.90, -20.8% YTD, +19.9% off the April 10 low of $20.19), and the print is the first real test of the SaaS-only guidance framework that triggered a -48.67% single-day gap on 2025-10-28 and a securities class action. The only number that matters is SaaS ARR ex-conversions: company guide is 27–28% in Q1 and 18–20% for FY26, calling for $120M of net-new organic SaaS ARR vs. $109.5M in 2025 — a +9.6% acceleration the Street is not pricing. FY26 EPS guide of $0.06 – $0.10 sits at roughly one-third of consensus ($0.21), so a maintained or raised FY guide forces a Street capitulation higher; any softness on conversions, FCF, or renewal commentary likely retests the $20 floor. Options market prices an 18.1% implied move (May 15 ATM straddle $4.60 / spot $25.41) — rich vs. 9.1% median historical realized but justified by the disclosure overhang and asymmetric downside skew (put IV 119 vs. call IV 102). Position size accordingly: this is a 25/50/25 wide-tail event, not a clean directional setup.

inflection_library.headline: Q3 2025 miss + slashed Q4/FY ARR outlook + announcement that on-prem self-hosted will be EOL'd by YE 2026; -48.67% single-day gap from $63.00 to $32.34, ~$3.8B of mcap erased.
Q4 2025 print: revenue and EPS beat, but FY26 EPS guide $0.06–$0.10 vs. $0.34 consensus + new SaaS-only KPI framework. Stock dropped -10.78%.
Closed AllTrue.ai (Altru) acquisition for ~$150M, extending platform from data security into AI agent governance/TRiSM.
